values: Local SQLite task command runtime and storage selection
As a CLI user or automation, I want task commands to operate on the intended local Cache so task lifecycle changes do not affect the wrong board.

Task commands parse global --db and open the Kanban runtime with that path; otherwise getDbPath uses KANBAN_DB_PATH, then existing ./.kanban/board.db, then existing ~/.kanban/board.db, then creates ./.kanban/board.db. SQLite opens with WAL and foreign keys. Local provider schema is initialized and default Columns are seeded for task commands unless the command is explicit board init.

Use isolated KANBAN_DB_PATH or --db for tests; unknown top-level flags throw INVALID_ARGUMENT; KANBAN_DEFAULT_COLUMNS can replace bootstrap Columns and rejects duplicate case-insensitive names; Postgres mode is out of scope.
Evidence: src/index.ts:26-57,342-346,374-376; src/provider-runtime.ts:30-33,56-61; src/db.ts:19-42,45-155; src/tracker-config.ts:87-120; README.md:65-90.
As a CLI user, I want to create a Task with structured fields so agents and scripts can act on it without guessing.

kanban task add <title> requires a title, passes -d, -c, -p, -a, --project, --label/--labels, and -m into provider.createTask, normalizes labels, validates priority, validates metadata JSON, defaults description/assignee/project to empty strings, labels to [], metadata to {}, priority to medium, and Column to backlog when -c is absent. New Tasks get t_ IDs, append at the end of the target Column, write created activity, enter column-time tracking, and return the created Task in a success envelope.

Missing title -> MISSING_ARGUMENT; bad priority -> INVALID_PRIORITY; invalid metadata -> INVALID_METADATA; unknown Column -> COLUMN_NOT_FOUND; comma-separated/repeated labels are trimmed and de-duplicated; empty labels are ignored; docs/code discrepancy candidates noted below.

Evidence: src/index.ts:40-41,80-97,423; src/db.ts:285-340; src/labels.ts:1-34; README.md:161,170-179; docs/workflow.md:42-61. Candidate D-P1-001: README.md:72 and src/tracker-config.ts:91-93 expose KANBAN_DEFAULT_TASK_COLUMN, but SQLite addTask defaults to backlog at src/db.ts:298. Candidate D-P1-002: code supports --label/--labels, CLI help documents --label, and README task add flags omit labels.
List, filter, sort, and limit Tasks
As a CLI user, I want to list Tasks and narrow them by workflow fields so I can choose the next work item.

kanban task list returns provider.listTasks in a success envelope. Filters: -c resolves Column by id or case-insensitive name, -p matches priority string, -a matches assignee exactly, --project matches project exactly. -l is parsed as a positive integer. --sort accepts priority, created, updated, position, or title; unknown sort values fall back to position. Default sort is position. Result rows include column_name from the join and hydrated labels.

Invalid/unknown Column -> COLUMN_NOT_FOUND; invalid limit strings, zero, negatives, decimals, exponent notation, and values over Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER -> INVALID_ARGUMENT; invalid priority filters are not validated before SQL and simply return no matches unless DB data matches; empty result returns [] in JSON or No items found. in pretty mode.
Evidence: src/index.ts:98-108,424; src/db.ts:356-409; src/transport-input.ts:12-30; src/output.ts:21-30,60-65; README.md:162,181-190; docs/workflow.md:64-72.
As a CLI user, I want to inspect one Task by id so I can see its current fields before acting.

kanban task view <id> requires an id and returns provider.getTask in a success envelope. The SQLite lookup joins the Task to its Column and throws TASK_NOT_FOUND when missing. The local provider enriches the Task with providerId, externalRef, url=null, assignees array, labels array, comment_count, version, and source_updated_at=null.
Missing id -> MISSING_ARGUMENT; unknown id -> TASK_NOT_FOUND. In this local SQLite slice only internal IDs are code-evidenced; external refs for Linear/Jira are out of scope.
As a CLI user, I want to update Task fields without recreating the Task so lifecycle state and history stay attached.

kanban task update <id> requires an id and passes --title, -d, -p, -a, --project, and -m to provider.updateTask. SQLite validates priority and metadata JSON, always updates updated_at and increments revision, updates only supplied fields, logs assigned/prioritized/project/title/description/metadata activity when values change, and returns the updated Task.

Missing id -> MISSING_ARGUMENT; unknown id -> TASK_NOT_FOUND before validation; bad priority -> INVALID_PRIORITY; invalid metadata -> INVALID_METADATA. Empty string values can be supplied for options parsed by parseArgs. Supplying no update fields still increments revision and updated_at because the UPDATE always includes those fields.
As a CLI user, I want to move a Task through workflow Columns so the board reflects progress.

kanban task move <id> <column> requires both id and target Column. SQLite resolves the target Column by id or case-insensitive name, no-ops when the Task is already in that Column, otherwise appends it to the target Column, renumbers the old Column positions, exits/enters column-time tracking, logs moved activity, increments revision, updates updated_at, and returns the moved Task.

Missing id or Column -> MISSING_ARGUMENT with usage; unknown Task -> TASK_NOT_FOUND; unknown Column -> COLUMN_NOT_FOUND; moving to the same Column returns the existing Task without revision/activity/time-tracking writes.
As a CLI user, I want a concise command to change a Task assignee during handoff.

kanban task assign <id> <user> requires id and assignee argument, then calls provider.updateTask(id, { assignee }). SQLite stores the assignee string, increments revision/updated_at, logs assigned activity only if the assignee changed, and returns the updated Task.

Missing id or omitted assignee -> MISSING_ARGUMENT with usage. An explicit empty assignee may be accepted through --style parser forms only if parseArgs yields an empty positional string; normal shell invocation without user is missing. Unknown Task -> TASK_NOT_FOUND.
As a CLI user, I want a concise command to set Task priority for planning.

kanban task prioritize <id> <level> requires id and priority level, then calls provider.updateTask(id, { priority }). SQLite accepts only low, medium, high, urgent, increments revision/updated_at, logs prioritized activity only if the priority changed, and returns the updated Task.
Missing id or level -> MISSING_ARGUMENT with usage; invalid priority -> INVALID_PRIORITY; unknown Task -> TASK_NOT_FOUND before validation because updateTask fetches the existing Task first.
As a CLI user, I want to remove a local Task when it is no longer needed.

kanban task delete <id> requires an id and calls provider.deleteTask. SQLite fetches the Task, exits column-time tracking, logs deleted activity with the old title, deletes the Task row, renumbers remaining Tasks in the old Column, cascades comments by schema, and returns the deleted Task in the success envelope.
Missing id -> MISSING_ARGUMENT; unknown id -> TASK_NOT_FOUND. Delete is available for local provider only per capabilities, but remote provider modes are out of scope for this slice.
Task command output and error formatting
As an automation or human CLI user, I want predictable output so results can be parsed or read directly.

Successful command handlers return { ok: true, data } and errors return { ok: false, error: { code, message } }. Default output is compact JSON. --pretty changes task arrays to one-line task summaries, Task objects to detail blocks, empty arrays to No items found., and errors to Error [CODE]: message. Unknown task action throws UNKNOWN_COMMAND.

The CLI process exits 1 for KanbanError and 2 for unexpected errors in the main branch. The exported run() function throws KanbanError rather than converting to an error envelope. Pretty Task detail omits empty assignee/project/description and omits metadata when it is {}.

values: Task command uses isolated KANBAN_DB_PATH on a fresh local SQLite Cache.
Create a temp directory; set KANBAN_PROVIDER=local, KANBAN_STORAGE=sqlite, KANBAN_DB_PATH=<tmp>/board.db; ensure repo .kanban and HOME DB paths are not the target.
Run task add, then inspect the temp DB for columns and the created Task.
Command succeeds; temp DB exists; PRAGMA journal_mode reports wal; default Columns are seeded; created Task is in backlog; no repo or HOME board path is used.
Security/data-integrity check for approved storage isolation rule.
--db selects the explicit SQLite path even when KANBAN_DB_PATH is also set.
Run task add with --db <tmp>/flag.db; inspect both files.
Task is stored in flag.db; env.db is absent or has no Task row from the command.
Protects against writing to unintended Cache.
KANBAN_DEFAULT_COLUMNS bootstraps custom Columns for task commands.
Run task add with -c Todo and task list -c Todo.
Task command succeeds and the seeded Columns are Todo, Doing, Done in positions 0,1,2.
CLI run() or subprocess plus sqlite inspection
Default board state is dependency evidence, not a board-admin feature.
Duplicate KANBAN_DEFAULT_COLUMNS names are rejected before ambiguous local Cache state is created.
Command fails with INVALID_CONFIG and no ambiguous duplicate Columns are created.
Unknown top-level CLI option is rejected before runtime work.
No DB should be needed; set KANBAN_DB_PATH to a temp path for safety.
Fails with INVALID_ARGUMENT and does not create or mutate the temp DB.
Security/perimeter check for strict argument parsing.
When no SQLite DB exists, task commands create local ./.kanban/board.db.
Run in a temp working directory with no .kanban/board.db and HOME set to a temp home without ~/.kanban/board.db; unset KANBAN_DB_PATH; set KANBAN_PROVIDER=local and KANBAN_STORAGE=sqlite.
Command succeeds and creates ./.kanban/board.db under the temp working directory; HOME DB remains absent.
CLI subprocess from temp cwd plus filesystem/sqlite inspection
Existing local ./.kanban/board.db is preferred over existing HOME DB.
Run in a temp working directory with existing local ./.kanban/board.db and HOME containing existing ~/.kanban/board.db; unset KANBAN_DB_PATH.
Task is inserted into the local working-directory DB, not the HOME DB.
CLI subprocess from temp cwd plus sqlite inspection
Protects against unintended HOME Cache mutation.
Existing HOME DB is used when local ./.kanban/board.db is absent.
Run in a temp working directory with no local .kanban/board.db and HOME containing existing ~/.kanban/board.db; unset KANBAN_DB_PATH.
Task is inserted into HOME ~/.kanban/board.db and no local ./.kanban/board.db is created.
CLI subprocess from temp cwd plus sqlite inspection
Minimal task add creates a Task with defaults.
Run task add "Minimal task"; view returned JSON and DB row.
Returns ok true with t_ id; title set; Column backlog; priority medium; empty description/assignee/project; labels []; metadata {}; revision 0; activity created and column-time entry exist.
task add preserves all structured flags.
Fresh isolated Cache with default Columns.
Run task add with -d, -c recurring, -p high, -a alice, --project Platform, repeated --label and comma labels, and -m valid JSON.
Returned Task has supplied description, Column, priority, assignee, project, normalized label array, and metadata string.
Label normalization trims, splits comma-separated input, ignores empty labels, and de-duplicates in first-seen order.
Run task add with --label "alpha, beta" --labels " beta,,gamma " --label alpha.
task add without title is rejected.
Run task add with no title.
Fails with MISSING_ARGUMENT and message includes Task title is required.
Invalid priority is rejected when creating a Task.
Fails with INVALID_PRIORITY; no Task row is inserted.
Invalid metadata JSON is rejected when creating a Task.
Fails with INVALID_METADATA; no Task row is inserted.
Unknown target Column is rejected when creating a Task.
Fails with COLUMN_NOT_FOUND and no Task row is inserted.
KANBAN_DEFAULT_TASK_COLUMN behavior for SQLite is confirmed or cleared.
Run task add "Default task column check" without -c, then inspect returned Column.
Expected from docs: review; expected from current code evidence: backlog. Execution determines whether D-P1-001 is confirmed.
Public CLI help documents both supported label flag spellings.
Fresh isolated Cache path for run().
Help text includes both --label name and --labels names.
Added during Phase 4 remediation for D-P1-002.
task list returns all Tasks in default position order.
Fresh isolated Cache with three Tasks created across Columns.
Returns ok true array of all Tasks ordered by position within SQL result semantics.
Column, priority, assignee, and project filters narrow results.
Create Tasks with distinct Column, priority, assignee, and project values.
Run task list with -c, -p, -a, and --project individually and in a combined matching case.
Each filter returns only matching Tasks; combined filters apply AND semantics.
Supported sort fields order Tasks as documented by code.
Create Tasks with different priority, titles, and timestamps or positions.
Run task list --sort priority, --sort title, --sort position, --sort created, and --sort updated.
Priority order is urgent, high, medium, low; title, position, created, and updated sorts use ascending SQL order.
Positive integer limit caps returned rows.
Run task list -l with 0, -1, 3.5, 1e3, 5abc, and a value greater than Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER.
Each invalid value fails with INVALID_ARGUMENT and a positive integer message.
Invalid priority filter value does not crash or mutate data.
Create one Task with a valid priority.
Returns ok true with an empty array under current code evidence.
Documents code behavior; not asserting docs because README only lists priority levels for creation/prioritization.
Listing with sort and limit remains responsive on a modest local board.
Create 100 local Tasks in isolated Cache.
Completes successfully within 2 seconds locally and returns 10 rows.
Updated-time sorting reflects Task mutation time.
Create two Tasks, update one after the other so updated_at values differ.
Rows are ordered by updated_at ascending under current SQL behavior.
run() plus sqlite inspection if timestamps tie
Specific coverage for the updated sort branch.
Unknown --sort falls back to position and does not error.
Create at least three Tasks in a known position order.
Command succeeds and returns Tasks in the same order as --sort position.
Covers Feature Ledger claimed fallback behavior.
task view returns a created Task with enriched local fields.
Create a Task with labels and metadata.
Returns the same Task id/title plus column_name, labels array, providerId/externalRef, url null, comment_count number, version string, source_updated_at null.
task view without id is rejected.
Run task view with no id.
Fails with MISSING_ARGUMENT and Task ID is required.
task view for an unknown id is rejected.
Pretty task view includes only non-empty optional fields.
Create a Task with assignee, project, description, and metadata, and another minimal Task.
Format both returned Task objects with --pretty path or formatOutput.
Full Task detail includes populated optional fields; minimal Task detail omits empty assignee/project/description and metadata {}.
formatOutput unit test or CLI subprocess
task update changes every supported field.
Run task update <id> --title, -d, -p urgent, -a bob, --project Infra, -m valid JSON.
Returned Task contains every supplied value; revision increments; updated_at changes or is refreshed.
task update changes only supplied fields.
Create a Task with populated fields.
Title changes; other fields remain unchanged; revision increments once.
task update with no field flags still follows current code behavior.
Create a Task and record revision.
Run task update <id> with no update flags.
Returns ok true with same user fields and revision incremented under current code evidence.
Potential UX friction if later judged undesirable; for now documents behavior.
task update requires an existing Task id.
Run task update without id; run task update t_missing --title X.
Missing id fails MISSING_ARGUMENT; unknown id fails TASK_NOT_FOUND.
Fails with INVALID_PRIORITY and stored Task priority remains unchanged.
Fails with INVALID_METADATA and stored metadata remains unchanged.
Changed fields write appropriate activity entries and unchanged fields do not.
Create a Task and count activity rows.
Update assignee, priority, project, title, description, and metadata; repeat same assignee/priority values.
Activity rows use assigned/prioritized/updated actions for changed fields only; repeated same assignee/priority do not add duplicate assigned/prioritized activity.
task move changes a Task Column and appends it to the target Column.
Create Tasks in backlog and recurring.
Run task move <id> in-progress; inspect returned Task and DB positions.
Task moves to in-progress, receives next target position, old Column Tasks are renumbered, revision increments, moved activity and column-time exit/enter are recorded.
Moving a Task to its current Column is a no-op.
Create a Task in backlog and record revision/activity/time entries.
Returned Task is unchanged; revision/activity/time-tracking counts do not change.
task move requires id and Column.
Run task move with no args and with only id.
Each fails with MISSING_ARGUMENT and usage text.
task move rejects an unknown Task id.
task move rejects an unknown target Column.
Fails with COLUMN_NOT_FOUND and Task remains in original Column.
task assign sets the assignee shortcut.
Returned Task assignee is carol; revision increments; assigned activity is recorded.
Assigning the same assignee updates revision but does not duplicate assigned activity.
Create a Task assigned to carol and count assigned activity rows.
Returned Task remains assigned to carol; revision increments under updateTask behavior; assigned activity count does not increase.
task assign requires id and assignee.
Run task assign with no args and with only id.
Each fails with MISSING_ARGUMENT and usage text.
task assign rejects an unknown Task id.
task prioritize sets a valid priority shortcut.
Returned Task priority is urgent; revision increments; prioritized activity is recorded.
Prioritizing to the same level updates revision but does not duplicate prioritized activity.
Create a Task with priority high and count prioritized activity rows.
Returned Task remains high; revision increments under updateTask behavior; prioritized activity count does not increase.
task prioritize requires id and level.
Run task prioritize with no args and with only id.
Each fails with MISSING_ARGUMENT and usage text.
task prioritize rejects an invalid priority level.
Fails with INVALID_PRIORITY and stored priority remains unchanged.
task prioritize rejects an unknown Task id.
Fails with TASK_NOT_FOUND before priority validation for valid priority input.
task delete removes a Task and returns the deleted Task.
Run task delete <id>; then task view <id>.

Deleting a Task renumbers remaining Tasks in the old Column.

task delete requires an existing id.

Missing id fails MISSING_ARGUMENT; unknown id fails TASK_NOT_FOUND.
Deleting a Task removes dependent comments through SQLite cascade.
Create a Task, then seed one dependent row directly into the comments table with that task_id.

No comments remain for the deleted Task.
Uses comment data only as dependent integrity state; comment feature behavior remains out of scope.
Default CLI output is compact JSON success envelope.

stdout parses as JSON with ok true and data object; no pretty-only text is present.
--pretty formats task list and task detail for humans.

Run task list --pretty and task view <id> --pretty.
List lines use priority icon/id/title/assignee/project; detail block includes expected fields and omits empty optional values.
Pretty empty arrays render as No items found.
Fresh isolated Cache with no matching urgent Tasks.

CLI process maps expected and unexpected failures to documented exit handling.
Run a KanbanError case such as task view t_missing; separately invoke a controlled unexpected-error path if feasible, otherwise waive with reason.
KanbanError process exits 1 and formats error envelope or pretty error; unexpected error exits 2 if a safe trigger exists.

KanbanError exit 1 and safe unexpected-error exit 2 branches executed. Unexpected branch uses --db pointing at a temporary directory, which causes INTERNAL_ERROR unable to open database file without touching user storage.

values: Reviewer approved revised slice: single-task/work-item lifecycle only, local Bun CLI only, isolated SQLite only; cautioned not to expand default-state setup into a feature family and to document any isolation harness work as prerequisite.

Read UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md before provider/API/task terms; used Task, Column, Cache, and Storage mode terminology. Read README.md, docs/readme.md, docs/workflow.md, src/index.ts, src/db.ts, src/provider-runtime.ts, src/tracker-config.ts, src/output.ts, src/transport-input.ts, src/labels.ts, src/providers/local.ts, src/providers/local-core.ts, src/providers/sqlite-local-store.ts, src/providers/types.ts, and related tests for evidence.

Comment commands, board/project/column administration, config mutation, bulk operations, server/API/UI/MCP/provider integrations, Postgres/shared deployment, and packaging/release flows recorded out of scope unless later approved as a new slice.
Phase 1 approved after revisions to CLI-TASK-010 exit evidence and D-P1-002 label-help wording.

Generated 60 behavior-focused test cases across all 10 Feature Ledger rows plus 3 end-to-end journeys, including happy path, error path, boundary, invalid input, data integrity, output, storage isolation, SQLite path resolution, automation JSON, human pretty output, and bounded responsiveness checks. Mobile/responsive is N/A for this CLI-only slice.
Phase 2 approved after revisions adding SQLite default path-resolution tests, created/updated/unknown sort coverage, direct-SQL comment cascade setup, and tighter WAL assertion.

Ran `bun test src/__tests__/cli-task-lifecycle-matrix.test.ts`; result: 63 tests executed, 62 passed, 1 failed, 627 assertions. Failed test: TC-002-08 expected KANBAN_DEFAULT_TASK_COLUMN=review to create the Task in review, actual column_name was backlog. TC-010-05 includes KanbanError exit 1 and safe unexpected-error exit 2 assertions.

Ran `rg -n "label|labels" src/index.ts README.md | head -20`; result: src/index.ts parser supports label and labels options, route passes both into labels, CLI help documents --label, and README task add flag table has no label flag rows.

Resolved D-P1-001 by passing local tracker config defaultTaskColumn into LocalProvider/SqliteLocalStore and using it for createTask only when input.column is absent. Files: src/providers/factory.ts, src/providers/local.ts, src/providers/sqlite-local-store.ts.
Resolved D-P1-002 by documenting --label and --labels in the README task add flag table and public CLI help. Files: README.md, src/index.ts. Added remediation regression TC-002-09.
Ran `bun test src/__tests__/cli-task-lifecycle-matrix.test.ts`; result: 64 passed, 0 failed, 631 assertions.

Ran related local CLI/provider tests: `bun test src/__tests__/index.test.ts src/__tests__/db.test.ts src/__tests__/local-provider-core.test.ts src/__tests__/tracker-config-columns.test.ts src/__tests__/local-provider-comment.test.ts src/__tests__/postgres-local-provider.test.ts`; result: 75 passed, 12 skipped, 0 failed.
Ran `bun test`; result: 539 passed, 27 skipped, 0 failed, 1872 assertions.
Ran `bun run typecheck`, `bun run ui:typecheck`, and source-scoped lint `bunx eslint . --ignore-pattern .claude/** --ignore-pattern outputs/**`; all passed.

Phase 4 approved. Reviewer confirmed D-P1-001 and D-P1-002 resolved, TC-002-09 meaningfully guards the public help surface, verification is sufficient, the bun run check limitation is acceptable for this slice, and no scope creep or architecture boundary issue was found.
Reran all approved Test Matrix cases and all 3 E2E journeys via `bun test src/__tests__/cli-task-lifecycle-matrix.test.ts`; result: 64 passed, 0 failed, 631 assertions.
Reran full repository tests via `bun test`; result: 539 passed, 27 skipped, 0 failed, 1872 assertions.
Reran `bun run typecheck`, `bun run ui:typecheck`, and source-scoped lint `bunx eslint . --ignore-pattern .claude/** --ignore-pattern outputs/**`; all passed.

`bun run check` was rerun during Phase 5 and still failed because repo lint scans generated/tooling paths under .claude/worktrees/serve-api-webhooks-qa/**, including dist/index.js and worktree scripts outside the project service. Source-scoped lint excluding .claude/** and outputs/** passed; the ledger generator itself also passes ESLint. This slice did not change global lint ignores.

Phase 5 approved. Reviewer confirmed regression scope complete, all user journeys passing, no open critical/high defects, and the bun run check limitation acceptably triaged outside touched source and approved-slice code.

Created fresh branch `codex/task-lifecycle-quality` from `origin/main` because the prior local branch `pr-76` diverged and would have included unrelated serve/webhook slice commits in a PR. Carried over only this slice worktree changes.
After committing on the fresh branch, reran `bun test src/__tests__/cli-task-lifecycle-matrix.test.ts`; result: 64 passed, 0 failed, 631 assertions.

After committing on the fresh branch, reran `bun test`; result: 559 passed, 27 skipped, 0 failed, 1935 assertions. Count differs from earlier Phase 5 evidence because `codex/task-lifecycle-quality` is based on current `origin/main`.
After committing on the fresh branch, reran `bun run typecheck`, `bun run ui:typecheck`, and source-scoped lint `bunx eslint . --ignore-pattern .claude/** --ignore-pattern outputs/**`; all passed.

After committing on the fresh branch, reran `bun run check`; it still failed with 100 lint errors under .claude/worktrees/serve-api-webhooks-qa/**. This remains outside the approved slice and outside the PR diff.

values: Task-detail collaboration is outside the approved single-task lifecycle slice even though comments are exposed through CLI/provider APIs.
Board initialization/view/reset are board administration or board read workflows; only default Column bootstrap is a dependency note.
Column CRUD is a separate board administration feature family.
Bulk move/clear are multi-task workflows and explicitly outside single-task lifecycle.
